name: COMPLETE.1b: Extension uniquely determined by analyticity
detail: Identity theorem: if two analytic functions agree on a set with a limit point in their common domain, they agree everywhere. So 1/(1-z) is the ONLY analytic continuation of Σz^n.

name: COMPLETE.1c: Without analyticity, extension is non-unique
detail: C^∞ functions: infinitely many smooth extensions exist. C^ω (analytic): exactly one extension. The constraint determines the result.

name: COMPLETE.2b: Completion Q→R is unique (up to isometry)
detail: Theorem: the completion of a metric space is unique up to isometry. Any two complete metric spaces containing Q as dense subset are isometric. This is COMPLETE, not EXTEND — the constraint (completeness) determines the result.

name: COMPLETE.3a: x²+1 has no real roots
COMPLETE.3b: x²+1 has roots in C (±i)
COMPLETE.3c: Algebraic closure is unique
detail: Theorem: algebraic closure exists and is unique up to isomorphism. This is COMPLETE — the constraint (every polynomial has a root) uniquely determines C from R.

name: COMPLETE.4a: Dedekind completion = Cauchy completion
detail: Both constructions produce isometric copies of R. Two different procedures, same unique result — strong evidence that COMPLETE is a real primitive, not an artifact of method.

name: COMPLETE.4b: Uniqueness is the structural signature of COMPLETE
detail: EXTEND: many possible results (choice involved). COMPLETE: exactly one result (constraint eliminates choice). The constraint (order-completeness OR metric-completeness) determines the same unique object from different approaches.

name: COMPLETE.5a: Tensor product is uniquely determined by bilinearity
COMPLETE.5b: Universal properties are instances of COMPLETE
detail: Free objects, tensor products, limits, colimits — all characterized by: given data + structural constraint → unique result (up to unique isomorphism). This is the categorical formalization of COMPLETE.

name: COMPLETE.6: Sheafification is COMPLETE
detail: Presheaf + gluing constraint → unique sheaf. The constraint (local sections must glue to global sections) determines the sheafification uniquely. Cannot verify in SymPy but well-established in algebraic geometry.
